description_long: This is a two-story brick house with a prominent front porch supported by white columns and detailed railings. The facade features symmetrical window placement with decorative trim and a gabled dormer above the entryway. The roof is a mansard style with gray shingles and two red brick chimneys. The side elevations are plain, with evenly spaced windows and a small rear extension. The rear of the house has a secondary entry with steps and a patterned tile landing. The overall design suggests a late 19th to early 20th-century American urban residential style, blending both decorative and functional elements.
description: A two-story brick house with a mansard roof, front porch, decorative trim, and symmetrical windows, typical of historic American urban neighborhoods.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry porch with white railings, columns, decorative trim, and three front-facing windows.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the plain brick wall, two windows, and the depth of the porch and roof.
description_view_2: Rear view with a small extension, secondary entry, patterned tile steps, and two rear windows.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with plain brick, two windows, and a clear view of the porch's side.
attribute_entryway_design: Central front door with steps, covered porch, white columns, decorative railings, and ornate trim above the entry.
